summary: Postinstall exfiltrates host identity and /etc/hosts to oast.fun OOB collaborator
details: package.json declares postinstall: node index.js, so installing this package automatically runs index.js on npm install. index.js collects the installer's hostname (os.hostname()), username, cwd, external IPv4, package name, and the contents of /etc/hosts (path assembled from four string fragments prt1='/e', prt2='tc', prt3='/h', prt4='osts' to evade static matching), then POSTs the JSON payload over plain HTTP to a unique attacker-controlled Interactsh OOB subdomain at lpzlajzjfkpfeefuzxbv6n5nob7bpuh6e.oast.fun/receive-data. The split-string construction of /etc/hosts alongside outbound POST is deliberate evasion, not incidental. There is no legitimate purpose for this behavior; the package's sole install-time effect is host reconnaissance and exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
